title: Ray Tracing Cores — RT Cores & BVH Traversal Units
description: Explains the dedicated hardware in NVIDIA Turing/Ampere/Ada (RT Cores) and AMD RDNA2/3 (Ray Accelerator) that accelerates BVH traversal and ray-AABB/ray-triangle intersection, and how this hardware interacts with standard shader execution for hit and miss shader invocations. RT core throughput and its relationship to BVH quality (SAH vs. LBVH) is a primary performance variable in hardware ray tracing workloads. Students will measure RT core utilization during a ray-traced shadow pass, compare SAH-built vs. LBVH-built BVH performance, and explain why TLAS refit is preferred to rebuild for dynamic objects.
